bio: Mortiholics are a party bar band that plays a weekly gig Every Wednesday at Mortimer's Bar. Besides the weekly gig they also play upon request elsewhere. Original Songs + Classics and Standards.

Honey Please is an indie pop rock band out of Minneapolis, Minnesota. Through a shared joy of musical expression and a love of performing, they have delighted many ears around the Twin Cities area. The band formed in the fall of 2022 and have been creating new tunes since. All original songs are written by singer-guitarist Sam Scheuneman who has been writing songs since he learned to speak. Bassist Dawson Ludvigson adds intricate bass melodies under a light melody sung by Sam. Guitarist Jack Nobel shreds lead lines to give each song its unique timbre. On the drums, Joe Kiser continues to keep beats fresh and alive with a lively and energetic tone. Together these musicians create a sound that feels complete and is full of emotion. When playing live the audience just cannot stop dancing and moving along to the beat. Honey Please released their first studio album in October '24.

Providing a soundtrack to the dystopian future we deserve.
The Real Chuck NORAD is a Minneapolis-based crew of cosmic groove engineers, blending experimental funk, trip-hop, and brass-fueled psychedelia into a dance soundtrack for an alternate universe. Like a spaceship crash-landing at a neighborhood block party, their sets are wild, sweaty, and otherworldly—equal parts interstellar mayhem and Minneapolis soul.

Oceanographer is an Indie Folk band from Minneapolis fronted by Val Lowry-Ortega. Oceanographer shines with influences of Laurel Canyon Folk, 90s Dream Pop, and Mid-2000's Emo. Their songs are lyric-forward and reverb-heavy, detailing the queer experience in small Midwestern towns. Oceanographer is for fans of: Faye Webster, Big Thief, Adrianne Lenker, The Sundays, Mazzy Star, Jeff Buckley, Weyes Blood, and Phoebe Bridgers. 

Oceanographer is a 2024 nominee for best upcoming artist at the Loring awards.

Izzy Cruz is a multi-instrumentalist songwriter that dives into deep grooves and deep human psyche. Cruz is drum-centrist and writes about topics of lived experiences to create an "otherworldly" vulnerably spiritual soundscape. After 12 years of gigging in various bands in the twin cities, Izzy has found heavy-hitting and light footed visionaries to join the extravagant train that is Izzy Cruz. Come with your hope, woes, whimsy and exactly where you are at, all are welcome and you are sure to live in a uniquely transient way of conceptualizing life.

Jon Peterson (guitar/vocals) started using the “Arcola” moniker nearly 20 years ago. Arcola has released two albums and now performs as a trio with Adam Pearson (bass) and Kelsey Welfald (drums). Some of their songs have been dubbed “cosmic Americana” and other songs are reminiscent of bands like Yo La Tengo.

Maybe it's because the band is fueled by cheap beer and quality whiskey, but when the Hamm Sammies set out to write country music, their groove strayed from the lines. They gravitated toward a more syncopated, swampy sound: country music played in between the cracks. With a little twang, some Memphis soul, plenty of blues, and even a dash of Sonny Rollins (especially his Way Out West era), their arrangements get people off their seats. If they're not careful, listeners might accidentally put aside their Midwestern stoicism for the duration of the Sammies' set.

The Hamm Sammies' debut album is available on vinyl and streaming services everywhere.

porch_address: 2441 Lyndale Ave S Minneapolis Mn Ave S
bio: Introducing, Fever Field: a funk pop rock band  from Uptown, Minneapolis. Founded by friends Jack Cassidy and Drew Lodermeier in 2022, You can find this 5-piece band playing local shows monthly throughout the cities and music on all streaming platforms! You can expect original music, spanning across different generes, as well as unique covers from some of your favorite artists. Stop by as Fever Field takes on their 3rd porchfest! Cheers!

If Pop Punk History Repeats Itself, This Band Just Might Be the Next Blink-182. Blink-182 burst onto the scene by touring with classic punk bands and gaining airtime on MTV, known for their on-stage antics and potty humor banter. Similarly, The Skinny Lovers have made their mark by being handpicked by Ramones drummer Marky Ramone for Sirius XM, and their first album was licensed by MTV.

The Skinny Lovers' lead singer can be spotted wearing his signature fedora and black rimmed glasses, while their stage banter is notorious for causing a stir. The resemblance to Blink-182 is uncanny without being a mere copy. Though their aesthetics differ, they share the same infectious vibe.

Their bassist sports a full mohawk reminiscent of a driver in Ubisoft's Crazy Taxi, his bromance with the lead singer is evident with irresistible banter as a mix of goofiness and charisma, and their drummer is the quiet, misunderstood type who absolutely wails on his instrument.

The recipe is there: the music, the energy, and the fans. This band is reviving the fun and chaos of live pop punk shows, and I’m all in for the ride.

Damn Phibian is not a band defined by boundaries. The four piece indie rock band draws influences from a wide range of musical ethos, including 70s Krautrock, 80s City Pop, and all of that good 90s Britpop. Add a large dose of funk and you get groovy beats, textured guitars, and funky time signatures that’ll get you dancing introspectively.

At the start of 2025 Damn Phibian collectively decided to donate the money they make from tickets to support local underprivileged groups in need, typically Twin Cities Trans Mutual Aid. We are fortunate to have enough to meet our needs and are happy to use our platform for good.

Damn Phibian has a busy year ahead of us full of sick shows and multiple recording sessions. Stay tuned ya funky frogs:D
Anthony Roos and Lisa Tradewell are knocking themselves out to come up with the catchiest songs and the sweetest harmonies they can muster.
The Denim Boys are The World's Most Popular Rock & Roll Band™. If you come to a Denim Boys show and don't have fun you can either sue them or come to their house, take your shirt off, and fight them.

At Lawnchair Dads, we are dedicated to rocking out while respecting the lawn decorations. Fueled by dad jokes, questionable dance moves, and an undying love for snacks, we promise to bring the ultimate backyard BBQ energy to every performance. Our mission? To deliver tunes so catchy they’ll make you put down your beer (but not for long), solos so smooth they rival a freshly mowed lawn, and vibes so chill they feel like a Sunday afternoon nap in a hammock.

Lawnchair Dads: Because standing is overrated, but rocking is mandatory.

Who is Art Beagle?  What does he do?  It seems like Art mostly sits in a chair, drinks beer and watches TV so why does he have a backup band?  

Never mind about that.

Art Beagle's Backup Band is a Minneapolis based trio with original music and explosive takes on familiar songs, featuring the talents of...

Michael Burton (Keys/Guitar/Vocals)
Tamir Nolley (Bass/Saxophone)
Yoji Sera (Drums)

From RnB to Funk to Pop, Jazz and even a little Butt Rock, Art Beagle's Backup Band is more than just great music.  It's banter, silly wigs, themes, weird hats, coloring books and more.  It's an experience!  You might even catch sight of Art Beagle himself!
The Saint Paul based The Get-Up Mondays plays melody-driven original tunes with influences from rock, honky-tonk, and bluegrass.
prettyprettypleasemusic.com

Some bands are born overnight. Others take their time—waiting, refining, and building anticipation.

Adam Szczepaniak and Jeremy Montoya had over two albums’ worth of songs ready to unleash in early 2020. Then, the world hit pause. With no band and no stage, they did the only thing they could: Szczepaniak wrote music. Montoya wrote lyrics. And together, they stockpiled a sound waiting to erupt.

In 2021, they took the best of the best into the studio with Wally Joseph in Andover, MN. Tracks were recorded. Photos were taken. Social media accounts were launched. And then… they waited. Again.

Fast forward to late 2024: Enough was enough. It was time. Szczepaniak and Montoya assembled their crew—Matt Greeninger on bass and Jonathan Kennedy on guitar—and hit the ground running.

Their debut single and video, “You Want It,” dropped in January 2025, instantly turning heads with its raw energy and infectious hooks. With a steady stream of singles lined up for the year and beyond, the wait is officially over.

This is just the beginning.

Zola grew up in San Francisco and has called Minneapolis home since 2021. As a self-taught musician and songwriter, she traces her artistic origins back to theater, classical voice, and creative writing. Strongly influenced by the expressiveness and musicianship of songwriters like Elliott Smith, Aimee Mann, and Lana Del Rey, Zola's sound lies somewhere vaguely in the realm of acoustic indie pop. She is left-handed, vegetarian, and obsessed with her 15-year-old cat. Find her Instagram and SoundCloud at @zola.rose.music.

Zoë Says Go delivers a compelling fusion of Americana and rock, weaving socially conscious themes into tales of love, struggle and wonder. Two of the band’s albums, “Turn the Table” and “Space Between the Notes” have landed in the top 100 on the US College Radio Charts. Their sound ranges from funky to joyous to tender and contemplative, influenced by the likes of Dylan, Springsteen, Peter Gabriel and U2. All proceeds from the band’s album sales benefit Unite 2 Fight Paralysis https://u2fp.org
Metal band from Minneapolis. We take our inspiration from bands like Cannibal Corpse, Death, early Slipknot, Slayer, and Suffocation. Come get rowdy with us!
It started with a summer, and it ended with forever.  Atomic Lights began in the Summer of 2012 as a way for three best friends to play rock and roll as fast and loud as they could.

The Up North Rollers Brass Band pays homage to the modern second line music of New Orleans. The group, led by trombonist Ben Collicott, began with a handful of Michigan Tech alumni, and has since grown to include musicians with varying backgrounds across the Twin Cities’ music community. With reverence to all aspects of New Orleans’ brass band culture, the Up North Rollers present an exciting musical experience rarely seen outside of the Crescent City.

A sonic and visual experience, MoonzOut formed in 2024 to create a unique chimera of jazz, metal, ska-punk, psychedelic, and some other bits thrown into the batch. Dancing and juggling round-off the guaranteed good time by this 5-piece ensemble. Come for some fun times with friends, y'all!

Larry Wish has been composing indie avant-rock music since 2007. With over fifty albums, including releases on labels such as Orange Milk Records, Moon Glyph, & Bumpy, his songs often explore daily observations, reflections, interactions, and trying to understand love and life without analyzing either to death. By combining different elements across the musical landscape, Larry Wish creates a musical universe that blurs the lines between harmony and dissonance. Larry has collaborated with artists such as David Liebe Hart from Adult Swim's Tim and Eric Awesome Show, Great Job!, has appeared on an album by the godfather of home recording, R. Stevie Moore, and has been featured by publications such as The Wire, Third Man Records' Maggot Brain, and Tiny Mix Tapes.

In a live performance, he may surreally mime a one person band of hyper-orchestral music, sing karaoke-styled songs which showcase his dramatic, low-register singing, play intimate piano tunes delivered through a wide emotional lens, or deliver a once-in-a-lifetime performance - never to be repeated! With the inclusion of a live band, a new dimension of sound and vision is added through the interpretation of well-seasoned players.
Meatraffle SKA features wonderful guitar, vocals & a fantastic horn section, as the band skips through first wave ska & reggae influences with an occasional new twist on pop covers.

Blending his prog-rap, west coast, and boom bap influences, Tune brings a fresh take on modern hiphop while exploring a wide range of themes and sub genres. Any beat, any style and any theme. Tune is the Swiss Army knife of hip hop and he’s bringing his unique sound to the twin cities.

Squinny is a Minneapolis psych fuzz glam four-piece. Synth and riff-heavy, the group’s high-energy performances are home to both dancing and head-banging. The combination of crushing rhythms and giant melodies is reminiscent of T. Rex a la Justice.

Good Morning Midnight are a homegrown Midwest indie rock band in the truest sense.

Initially formed as the songwriting vehicle of Charlie Cacciatore, GMM quickly grew into a full-fledged ensemble with a rotating lineup of local musicians filling out its ranks. Lo-fi home recordings blossomed into full-band arrangements on the band’s 2017 debut Basket of Flowers, with Cacciatore's open-hearted lyrics and jangly Telecaster licks evoking indie heroes of yore such as The Replacements, Sebadoh, and Built to Spill.

From then on, Good Morning Midnight have kept up a steady and prolific pace both in the studio and on the regional touring circuit. Each subsequent album — 2018's Both Neither and Both, 2021's Songs of Violence, and the 2024 offering All the Birds' Men — depicts a different iteration of GMM. To use the analogy of film, the cast and the scenery shift and alter, but the auteurist eye of the director remains constant.

In this case, Cacciatore’s direct and personal style of songwriting and undeniable guitar chops are the common thread that runs throughout Good Morning Midnight and its many chapters. Here is a rare band that taps into both the literary intellect and the classic rock impulse to shred through the warmth of a vintage tube amp.

The band is currently based out of Minneapolis, MN.

The Customers are a Minneapolis rock and roll band formed in the mid ‘90s. After signing with Neil Young’s Vapor Records the band toured with Ben Folds Five, The Meices, played festivals in Europe featuring Young and Pearl Jam, Soundgarden, and Foo Fighters; opened shows for Bad Religion, Soul Asylum, and supported Iggy Pop on several West Coast dates. Television appearances include ABC in Concert and The Conan O’Brien Show. The band is currently in the studio recording the EP “LIVING LIKE GODS,” a follow up to the 2023  Kevin Bowe-produced LP, “SWEET FATALITY.”

Based in Minneapolis, Minnesota, 18-year-old singer-songwriter FINICK (real name Josie Hasnik) creates moving, whimsical indie folk music. FINICK expertly balances their real-life emotions with shimmering, imaginative narratives of their own creation. The result is a powerful, unique, and compelling sound reminiscent of inspirations like Brandi Carlisle, Florence + The Machine, and Phoebe Bridgers. With their songs, FINICK hopes to reach the people who seek escape through music.

Jazicality is an R&B/ Pop band that melds together elements of multiple genres such as jazz, hip hop, and rock to express their motivational lyrics. Their music empowers you with a therapeutic experience that uplifts you.

Tony was the weird kid with the tall tales, who played as a one-man-band at his high school talent show (guitar, harmonica, bass drum, hi-hat). After growing up overseas, he was a character in the New Wave music scene in Tulsa, OK in the mid 80s. The recent gift of an accordion and the revelations of a capo, have led to a renaissance of creative longing to make new music and perform live again. He has a special place in his heart for folk songs, and anything old-timey. He enjoys making videos with his fearless cats, and occasionally lighting himself on fire while riding a unicycle.
We are a band of 3 Native Americans from different tribal heritage backgrounds based out of Minneapolis Minnesota who have come together to make epic original rock music!!

Catchy songs with darker themes. Pain. Loss. Regret. 

RIYL Against Me!, Lawrence Arms, Off With Their Heads.

Short songs that beg you to sing along. 

Couch Potato Massacre is a super fun MPLS quartet (Bass, Drums, Guitar, and Keys) that writes catchy little  songs about TV Shows, Movies, and Video Games. 
It’s Pop Culture Pop Punk! 

RIYL: Groovie Ghoulies, House Boat, Masked Intruder, Pkew Pkew Pkew, Ramones.

HUNNY BEAR is a funk/fusion quartet located in Minneapolis, MN. Featuring a combination of styles and performing music ranging from modern covers, funk standards, fresh sounds and originals, everyone is sure to enjoy the cool vibes and energy at any performance.

Waterline the brainchild of four longtime friends and collaborators Will Quie (Guitar, Vocals), Elliot Hicks (Keyboard, Vocals), Henry Ricker (Drums) and Sean Clements (Bass). With friendships forged in elementary school, this group of musicians have been playing music together in various formats and lineups their whole lives. “We played school events, bars, basements, backyards and everywhere in between” says frontman Will Quie, “we were always looking for a place to play.” Both Elliot Hicks and Will Quie attended the Saint Paul Conservatory for Performing Arts and went on to pursue conservatory and musical theatre degrees. In addition to playing in Waterline, Sean Clements is the founding member of the acclaimed hardcore band KillusOnline. Henry Ricker is the percussionist and one of the chief songwriters of the group. Their all original music is a stylish blend of Jazz Fusion, Alternative Rock and Psychedelia managing to be simultaneously danceable and introspective. Fans of the band's music often compare their eclectic sound to the lush jazz rock instrumentation of Steely Dan, the fluid harmonies of Chon and the lyrical surrealism and synth textures of Radio-Head. Waterline in its present iteration, has been performing for two years, playing all over the Twin Cities, from Surly Brewing to the White Squirrel Bar. In May of this year they will be releasing their first single “Nightline”, an instrumental voyage to a blade runner-esque future.

MY COUSIN DALLAS was born in a Wisconsin college radio station, took their first steps out of a coffeehouse series to spend the next two decades giving the Midwest a good answer to the question, 'What exactly is Garage Rival?'
Best described as "The Replacements and The Barenaked Ladies getting together and covering Duran Duran; a performance by My Cousin Dallas is an eclectic adventure using 20+ years of originals and unique covers to make sure everyone is having a fantastic time

Burning Blue Rain is the bluesy, funky rock-n-roll project of lifelong friends Louis Tessman Stanoch and Oliver Osland. With gritty vocals, soulful guitar, and rich keyboard and organ tones, their live shows are dynamic and immersive. Their 2025 EP "Blue Frame" is earning attention from fans and critics.
https://www.instagram.com/monarch.twincities

kvsket blends an expansive range of rock and metal stylings, transitioning from crushingly heavy to seductively intimate, often within the same song. Formed by four friends who, all looking for something more outside of their former bands and projects, came together to weave their sprawling influences and inspirations into a unique, raw and personal sound.

The Argyle Street Trio is a dynamic vocal-centric ensemble comprising three versatile Twin Cities singers: Rhiannon Fiskradatz, Kristen Olsen Rodriguez and Dale Dahlquist.

Featuring rich 3-part vocal harmony, they’ll have you tapping your toes and singing along - seated, standing or dancing. Their shows appeal to all ages.

Dale, a veteran guitarist in the Twin Cities music scene since the 1980s, provides solid guitar accompaniment and solo work. In addition to killer vocals, Rhiannon and Kristen add creative hand percussion, including cabasa, tambourine and hand drum.

A diverse repertoire includes classic Western swing, Chicago blues, Big Band & Hot Club jazz, and a treasury of your favorite vintage rock tunes. On the acoustic side, they range to Americana, folk, Irish and bluegrass, and instrumentals with elements of different genres.

They create arrangements that capture the essence of masterful composers, including The Beatles, Eric Clapton, Indigo Girls, Mills Brothers, Janis Joplin, Cole Porter, Joni Mitchell, John Prine, Bonnie Raitt, Sons of the Pioneers, Dolly Parton, Crosby-Stills-Nash, Paul Simon, Linda Ronstadt, Frank Sinatra, ABBA, Taylor Swift, Los Lobos, Adele and Bob Wills & The Texas Playboys.

When they add award-winning bassist Dik “His Lowness” Hedlund (Bonnie Raitt, A Prairie Home house band, Chuck Berry), you have a robust quartet with a powerful sound.

Listen:   	www.argylestreettrio.com

Contact:	dale@daledahlquist.com
Bad Ives plays rock & roll music. They're good-bad, but they're not evil.

Wood Hoops is an upbeat jazz-funk-rock band that knows how to keep the beat and move your feet! Featuring two boisterous lead horns, a silky smooth lead vocalist, and a rhythm section that will sear your ears and make your feet sore from boogying, don't miss your chance to party with one of the liveliest stage acts to come out of Omaha, Nebraska!

Did you get that dream we sent you? Powder Horn Park local band, Dream Vandals is an eclectic mix of indie, funk, punk, and R&B. As musical explorers, we traverse niche soundscapes in an attempt to push boundaries and blur genres. Our sound will spirit you away to ethereal planes rarely ventured.

Blending soul and folk into a sound that's both heartfelt and laid-back, The Color Cold traces its roots back over a decade. It all started with two high school friends— Ellie, vocalist and songwriter, and Sarah, guitarist, vocalist, and co-writer—sharing songs and harmonies in basements and bedrooms. Their collaboration grew over the years, eventually evolving into a full band with the addition of Sam on drums, Alex on piano, and Josh on upright bass.

Together, we create music that ranges from deeply personal originals to reimagined covers—each performance focused on making listeners feel something real. Our style is soulful, folky, and full of intention, but never too serious. More than anything, we value connection, community, and the joy of playing live music.
3-piece grungy, synthy alternative rock with an acceptable amount of howling vocals that you can shout along with, if you so desire.

Joan of Profile is a versatile female-fronted band with songs that have strong story-telling lyrical content set to the musical background of a modern alternative sound with pop sensibilities and energy. The members each bring unique influences to the music from folk, to hard rock, to country which all help to create a dynamic live set that keeps an audience engaged. Joan of Profile is going to rise to the occasion and attract attention by treating every show as special.

Playing traditional jazz and blues from the 1890's to the 1920's-America's original art form, will get your feet moving! The Pig's Eye Jass (the original spelling)
Band has been going since 1961. The band's approach is to study and play the classic pieces in the roots of American popular music, familiar and less well-known, celebrating but not necessarily imitating the originals: King Oliver, Jelly Roll Morton, Louis Armstrong, Sidney Bechet, etc. Nancy Hite, sings in the style of blues icons such as Bessie Smith. Take a trip back to the beginning of it all.

La Crosse’s Charlieboy is an innovative indie rock outfit that defies genre expectations with its honest, abstract, and therapeutic sound. Rooted in the Midwest, the band channels raw emotion into intricate, spontaneous grooves that blend elements of indie rock, jazz, pop, and prog. Their sound marries a cutthroat intensity with moments of delicate vulnerability—evoking the unexpected comfort of a tender embrace amid life’s harsher edges. This unique sonic palette not only challenges conventional musical norms but also paints a vivid picture of both struggle and hope, all while staying unapologetically true to themselves and their art.

The Midnight Current is an eclectic power trio from Minneapolis with foundations in blues, folk, and unhinged improvised psychedelic rock. Fronted by vocalist/guitarist Andy Caron, their sounds capture the sentiment of love, intensity of fear, and the satire of modern times.

Teenage Superman is an indie/synthpop artist from Mankato, MN. He has been writing music since the age of 8, initially inspired by the works of Chopin and Tchaikovsky. 

Since using the moniker of Teenage Superman (since high school - go figure), he has pivoted towards more electronic sounds. What started as demos intended for a full band, then morphed into a raw, 16-bit inspired electronic sound.

His most recent album, "endless innocence", was released in August of 2024, and includes songs such as "when all is said and done" and "minnesota", which have received airplay on college stations across the country.

Pity Party is a Minneapolis based indie alternative band that is redefining nostalgia. They were formed in 2024 and have already played at Green Room, Amsterdam, and more. They are stoked to be back at Porch Fest alongside all of these great local bands! Make sure to be on the lookout for new music soon.

For fans of: Mitski, Paramore, Chapell Roan

Cain & Co. combines good old Southern rock 'n' roll with that sweet Blues heat. Led by Michael Cain, this five piece our house draws in inspiration from groups like the Allman Brothers, Larkin Poe, Blackberry Smoke and Kingfish. If your inner redneck has a little southern drawl, this is the band for you.

Singer-songwriter Avery Seed needs only an acoustic guitar to emphasize her diaristic lyrics and breezy voice to honestly convey the annoyances of being an emotionally susceptible teenage girl. Her songs convey the organic quality that could only be achieved by being written in her bedroom, similar to Gracie Abrams, Annika Bennett, and Grace Enger. Seed is currently a freshman at Berklee School of Music and gigging around the Twin Cities when she is home. Avery is currently working on growing her social media presence and releasing music.

In my songs, you'll find a blend of introspective folk narratives and authentic emotional expression, reflecting the Daoist principles of simplicity and harmony.

​Through a fusion of haunting melodies and thought-provoking lyrics, I offer a sound that resonates with those searching for meaning and connection in a fragmented world. My music stands out by weaving together personal vulnerability with universal themes, creating a shared space for introspection and healing.
Ray gun youth merge triumphant fuzzed out riffs with psychedelic textures and introspective lyrics for a unique emo-tinged swirling indie rock sound.

Often compared to a cross between Young the Giant, Vance Joy, and Hippo Campus, Spencer Douglas takes inspiration from folk and indie pop, making his sets a mix of upbeat and feel-good tunes along with more reflective stories.
His college graduation and latest single, Grow With Me, has marked a shift in Spencer’s discography, highlighting the changes he has experienced both musically and personally.
Begun in 1990 Mommy Log Balls has undergone several iterations…Do you ever Drive your Mom’s car…?

"The Skeleton Crew" is a 3year old Alt-Rock band from Mn that provides a sonic landscape that's alien, yet familiar. Arturo(front man) and crew come together to create a music feature...featuring...The Creature

ditch pigeons music feels like coming home 
mixing ethereal loop pedal and delicate but beautiful electric guitar with intense and thoughtfully crafted lyrics
All Original Power Trio based out of the Twin Cities playing classic rock guitar driven music.
a 4-piece assemblage of Twin Cities scene veterans and transplants, Peony Park craft heartfelt, dynamic post-punk strewn with deeply personal lyrics, catchy hooks, and hummable melodies.

band_name: Dee Langley and the Masked Accordions
bio: Dee Langley and the Masked Accordions is an eclectic accordion ensemble that plays family friendly, inclusive music that encompasses Italian love songs, Balkan dances, French musette, Ukrainian waltzes, Irish folk songs. We play classical music and rock, music from Broadway and music from Star Wars. The group is led by Dee Langley, a longtime professional accordionist and instructor who has appeared with the Minnesota Orchestra, St. Paul Chamber Orchestra, Houston Symphony Orchestra, Guthrie Theater, Duluth Symphony Orchestra, Minnesota Sinfonia, Minnesota Philharmonic Orchestra, Nautilus Music Theater, Ethnic Dance Theatre, Dolina Polish Folk Dancers and Theater Latte Da. Ms. Langley is winner of the 2012 Minnesota Emerging Composer Award (Orkestar Bez Ime - World Music), the 2011-2012 McKnight Fellowship for Performing Musicians. Langley is a member of the American Accordion Hall of Fame and is the founder and coordinator for the NE Accordion Festival.

IdlFlo (pronounced "idle flow") is a four-piece garage/alt-rock wrecking crew from Minneapolis. Built on raw grit and contrast, they shift from whisper-quiet to explosive in a heartbeat. Fueled by roaring riffs, huge hooks, and layered harmonies, their sound echoes the restless energy and angst of '90s alternative. Known for hard-hitting performances that leave a mark, IdlFlo has ditched the studio to bring the noise straight to Twin Cities stages. No backing tracks. No safety nets. Just raw original rock.

We are Headcase! A fresh and original Twin Cities local band. We have 6 members with ages ranging from 22-65. Our diverse age range calls for a fresh and unique take on rock. Blending punk and zeppelin-esque guitar styles with blues and jazz inspired drums and bass, Our music is a reflection of many eras and styles of rock dynamically blended together to form a cohesive and original sound. Originally founded by my father, brother and I, adding in three other family friends, we’ve easily become a great group of friends who love creating unique music and rocking out! We have a setlist of all original music, with the exception of a few covers and have our first self titled album “headcase” released this year.

Nat Harriet is a singer and songwriter from Minneapolis. She sings catchy, intimate confessionals on guitar and piano. With a catalog of secret songs written over the last decade, she’s finally ready to share.

Lead by Vittorio, born and raised in Sicily (Italy) but transplanted in Minneapolis for 17 years now, the Bridges’ music style has been defined as Mediterranean infused pop rock. Our sounds and lyrics bridge generations, cultures, geographies. Founded in 2021, their 30+ songs cover topics that borrow from both universal themes and personal experience.

Instumentally Chill

Hailing from the Twin Cities, Tinynormous brings an electrifying mix of instrumental jazz grooves, rock vibes, and jam band flow, all wrapped in a comfy robe of joy and passion.

Tinynormous stays true to their 'old-school' roots — no computers, no AI—focusing on genuine human connections and turning every performance into a joyful celebration.
Original music that spans multiple genres, including rock, pop, Americana, folk, and whatever else feels good at the moment.

DayFunk : It’s not about us—it’s about all of us.

We’re a community brought together by a love for house music. Whether you’ve been here since the beginning or just found your way in, welcome.

The legends who started it all are still out there, inspiring us. Every month, we honor them with events that are more than just a party. They’re a chance to connect, celebrate the music, and support the local artists keeping the sound alive.

Join us.

It’s all happening right now.

Hella Reptilian! Is the spectacle you can’t quite put your finger on. A band of lizards that leave your jaw on the floor and a mouth salivating with questions; What did I just witness? Can my head bang any harder? I’m stuffed to the gills on delicious riffage, yet… I’m hungry for more? With their unique flavor of punk/ metal, Hella Reptilian! Unravels these mysteries and more with a performance you have to see to believe. Let’s see if your eyes and ears are bigger than your musical stomach.

Fueled by a lifelong passion for music, combined with over 20 years of experience on the decks; The Sound Insurgent (aka T.S.I.) has a rich history of being a trusted local favorite. 

T.S.I. offers an eclectic and diverse selection of sounds, an expert knack for reading his crowd, and standout style that makes every performance a unique and energetic experience. 

Inspired as a teen to master the art of DJing, his learning began by playing vinyl records on turntables and has since evolved with several mixing platforms to keep with technology and industry standards. 

Paying much respect to all deejays and their love for the craft, as well as artists and producers alike, TSI welcomes the ongoing challenge of mastering his art. Specializing in 90’s-00’s hip-hop & RnB, T.S.I. is sure to get those memories flowing.

The Sound Insurgent has toured throughout the Midwest and deejayed for notable hip-hop acts: Lazer Rocket Arm (Sioux City, IA), D.A.D. (Sioux City, IA), TreyLane (Sioux Falls, SD) as well as others. Some notable openings for DJ’s such as Bear Grillz and Jayceeoh as well as acts such as Twista, Nelly, Sublime with Rome, and Warren G, Ludacris and Dj for Ally Brooke.  

T.S.I. has performed at festivals like Jazz Fest (Sioux Falls, SD) Saturday in the Park (Sioux City, IA’s biggest music festival) , Hard Rock (Sioux City, IA) , Battery Park (Hard Rock, Sioux City, IA) and held residency at Red’s Bar, Tom Foolery’s, Key Club and a number of guest spots for various live events.

He has also shared his wealth of DJ expertise through classes at the Sioux City Conservatory of Music.

Emily Boyajian is a composer who uses her strong background in music theory to write music that is intellectually stimulating and emotionally captivating. She has written solo piano works, chamber music, orchestral music, and art songs. She has had her music performed at the 2019 Source Song Festival, the 2021 and 2023 Film Score Fest, the 2023 Cedar Commissions, and in other concerts in the Twin Cities. Her songs focus on topics such as her experiences as a trans woman, contemporary political and social issues, and silly songs about things such as Delta Airlines and arguing with a sock puppet.

Not Just For Halloween Anymore… King Sized Coffin writes songs about love and death and monsters. The forthcoming album, “Midnight,” explores themes of loss, grief, and reunion. For fans of Nick Cave, Danny Elfman, Harley Poe, and Tom Waits.

With three vocalists, two acoustic guitars, and one violin, Bee and the Knees shares original folk pop tunes, traditional Indian folk songs, and unexpectedly mellow covers. Ritika Ganguly and Charlie McCarron are the lead lyricists, with Shinjan Sengupta providing intricate classical guitar riffs and Celeste Kiewel gluing everything together with soaring violin melodies.
